title: A slow or short Maps run is usually Google throttling, not failure (tiered cooldowns)
summary: The Maps worker has tiered ban-detection cooldowns: consent wall=0s, reCAPTCHA=30s, CAPTCHA form / 'unusual traffic'=120s, 403/429/503 error page=300s. CAPTCHAs are auto-solved (2Captcha) when configured. A run that takes minutes or returns fewer than max_results is normally throttling, not a bug — and partial results are persisted incrementally so you keep what was scraped. Set the user's timing expectation and never tight-loop on status.
references: docs/services/SpiderBrowser/LEARNINGS.md:233-237 — incremental result persistence (avoid losing a partial scrape)
